title: Meeting Card v1 (shared)
description: One JSON per meeting. This schema is shared with the meeting-coach companion: project-assistant uses the factual layer (context, attendees, decisions, action-card links); meeting-coach writes the reflection layer (analysis). The two never collide — they own different fields. Lives in <project>/meetings/.

id.description: Format: MTG-{customer_code}-{project_code}-{YYYY-MM-DD}-{NN}. NN is a 2-digit ordinal for multiple meetings on one date in the same project; otherwise 01. The {customer_code}-{project_code} portion must match the linked project-card id.

purpose.description: Why did the group come together? 1-3 sentences. Hard non-empty.

desired_outcome.description: What did this specific meeting need to produce or move forward? One sentence.

attendees.description: People present. When contact_id is set, the contact-card is the authoritative source; name/role/party here are convenience copies.

decisions.description: project-assistant layer. Decisions taken in the meeting. Factual record, not reflection.

decided_by.description: Who held decision authority for this one.

action_cards_created.description: project-assistant layer. Action-card ids spun out of this meeting. Closes the loop between a meeting and the work it generated.

notes.description: project-assistant layer. Free-text factual notes or a short summary. NOT a transcript.

user_intent.description: meeting-coach layer. What YOU wanted to land in this meeting, written before any reflection. Required by meeting-coach before it will analyze.

transcript_excerpt.description: meeting-coach layer. Short excerpt for identification, NOT the full transcript.

analysis.description: meeting-coach layer. Written by the reflection companion, not by project-assistant. See the meeting-coach schema for the full shape (strengths, growth_points, reflection_questions, pattern_read).

user_reflections.description: meeting-coach layer. Append-only log of the user's reasoning.

status.description: logged = factual record captured by project-assistant; analyzed = meeting-coach produced reflection; archived = closed.
